title: Mastering Async Closure State Updates in React 18 & 19
excerpt: A deep dive into how asynchronous state updates inside timers and event listeners close over mutable variables by reference, and how to fix them using block-scoped constants.
content:
In modern React, state updates are batched asynchronously to maximize rendering performance. While this optimization works perfectly for standard user interactions, it introduces subtle and deceptive closure bugs when paired with timers (setInterval or setTimeout) or global event listeners.

The Closure Bug

When you define an interval inside a useEffect hook, the callback closes over the lexical scope of that effect hook. If you reference a block-scoped mutable index counter (like a let currentIdx = 0) directly within your state setter callback:

setLogs((prev) => [...prev, fullLogs[currentIdx]]);
currentIdx++;

The setter callback (prev) => ... captures currentIdx by reference. When React eventually flushes the batched state updates, the loop has already incremented currentIdx past the array boundaries, producing undefined values and crashing the application.

The Immutable Scoping Solution

To secure state updates from closure reference drift, you must capture the array element inside an immutable const binding at the exact tick the update is queued:

const logToAppend = fullLogs[currentIdx];
if (logToAppend !== undefined) {
  setLogs((prev) => [...prev, logToAppend]);
}
currentIdx++;

Because logToAppend is declared fresh inside each interval block scope, the React state callback closes over that unique immutable value, preserving state consistency perfectly!

title: Building Production-Ready REST APIs with Django & FastAPI
excerpt: How to choose between the robustness of Django REST Framework and the lightning speed of FastAPI, complete with dockerization tips.
content:
Python is an exceptional language for modern backend services, offering two world-class frameworks for API development: Django (DRF) and FastAPI. Choosing the right one for your product depends on your team's administrative structure and performance targets.

Django REST Framework: The Batteries-Included Titan

For data-intensive SaaS applications, ERP modules, and corporate dashboards, Django REST Framework is unmatched. Its built-in ORM, secure admin portals, migration managers, and automated authentication layers allow a single engineer to build complete secure web portals autonomously.

FastAPI: Lightning Speed and Modern Typings

When engineering high-throughput microservices, real-time geolocation telemetry pipelines, or lightweight serverless functions, FastAPI is the premier choice. Underpinned by Starlette and Pydantic, it provides asynchronous loop handling, native type-validation, and instant automatic OpenAPI documentation.

Dockerization for Seamless Deployments

No matter which framework you select, running inside a standardized Docker container is essential for remote engineering teams:

FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

Using containerized builds ensures your APIs execute identical runtime structures from local testing coordinates all the way to cloud production clusters.
